gaps_to_close_before_broad_public_launch: real external first-user outcome lift is not yet statistically proven
network effects are not yet proven with independent external agents
broad non-Python rescue coverage is intentionally limited/fail-closed
served-runtime MCP reload must be verified in each host environment before claiming that host is green

security_release_blockers: critical secret reaches shared atom store
high-risk PII reaches shared atom store
learning atom publish path uses no-op scanner fallback

security.local_default: local-first; shared memory path accepts signed, sanitized, revocable learning atoms
security.pii_raw_trace_policy: raw traces/conversations/tool outputs/source/env are not acceptable shared-memory payloads
security.prompt_injection_policy: scan and neutralize retrieval poisoning/tool coercion/instruction override before retrieval/export
residual_risk: scanner coverage is deterministic and test-backed, not a mathematical proof of no PII
untrusted packs still require source/trust verification and safety scan context
host MCP configs must set the correct absolute BORG_HOME to avoid split-brain stores

value.expected_time_to_visible_value_seconds: 30-90 for a known Python/Django class after install
value.expected_value_type: avoided debugging dead-end, next command/check, verification step, outcome feedback path
value.first_visible_value_path: pip install agent-borg -> borg rescue '<error>' -> ACTION/STOP/VERIFY + human receipt
value.north_star_metric: percent of first sessions where the user can name one specific dead-end Borg prevented
value.proven_example: ModuleNotFoundError maps to missing_dependency with tested confidence and 42/45 seed evidence in the rescue smoke

verdict.why: local gates, security surface, first-user surface, and rescue surface are green; external outcome lift remains unproven
